title: Failing Trie Insert Due to Node Creation Issue

statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat:
Input will be provided in the following format:
First line: Number of elements to insert
Second line: Space-separated element values
statement.outputFormat: For each inserted element, print "Inserted" on a separate line, followed by the inserted element's value.
statement.notes: Use a 26-way Trie with 0-based index for letters 'a' to 'z'.

examples.explanation: Insert the word 'hello' into the Trie

hints.text: Use a separate Node class to encapsulate child nodes and the character at each position.

editorial.title: Correcting Trie Insert Failure
editorial.intuition: Create a new node for each character in the input string and append it to the corresponding child node in the Trie.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach summary: Create a new node for each character in the input string and recursively update all child nodes.
editorial.betterSolution: Use a Node class to encapsulate child nodes and the character at each position. This will simplify the process of adding new nodes to the Trie.
editorial.optimalSolution: Optimal solution: Use a Node class to encapsulate child nodes and the character at each position. Create a new node for each character in the input string and append it to the corresponding child node in the Trie.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Correctness proof: The Node class correctly encapsulates child nodes and the character at each position. The Trie implementation correctly inserts new nodes and characters.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run: Insert the word 'hello' into the Trie using the Node class and Trie implementation.
